Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Ada 2022 41 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. Analytic Fog Rendering with Volumetric Primitives (2025) 72 upvotes, 3 comments
  3. Entomologists use a particle accelerator to image ants at scale 63 upvotes, 6 comments
  4. Apache Otava 27 upvotes, 1 comments
  5. Supertoast tables 42 upvotes, 5 comments
  6. Payphone Go 250 upvotes, 56 comments
  7. CT Scans of Health Wearables 148 upvotes, 31 comments
  8. Xous security focused open source on 22nm custom silicon 64 upvotes, 11 comments
  9. Paul Brainerd, founder of Aldus PageMaker, has died 102 upvotes, 21 comments
  10. Hardening Firefox with Anthropic's Red Team 354 upvotes, 107 comments
  11. TypeScript 6.0 RC 9 upvotes, 0 comments
  12. Anthropic, Please Make a New Slack 22 upvotes, 3 comments
  13. LibreSprite – open-source pixel art editor 214 upvotes, 73 comments
  14. Triplet Superconductor 21 upvotes, 3 comments
  15. Astra: An open-source observatory control software 63 upvotes, 17 comments
  16. Good Bad ISPs 78 upvotes, 25 comments
  17. Open Camera is a FOSS Camera App for Android 161 upvotes, 73 comments
  18. Workers who love ‘synergizing paradigms’ might be bad at their jobs 442 upvotes, 252 comments
  19. A tool that removes censorship from open-weight LLMs 51 upvotes, 21 comments
  20. Show HN: Moongate – Ultima Online server emulator in .NET 10 with Lua scripting 186 upvotes, 109 comments
  21. Show HN: A trainable, modular electronic nose for industrial use 23 upvotes, 8 comments
  22. 70k Books Found in Hidden Library in This Germany Home (2023) 52 upvotes, 25 comments
  23. Tech employment now significantly worse than the 2008 or 2020 recessions 424 upvotes, 282 comments
  24. It took four years until 2011’s iOS 5 gave everyone an emoji keyboard 115 upvotes, 67 comments
  25. Why it takes you and an elephant the same amount of time to poop 29 upvotes, 13 comments
  26. Show HN: Claude-replay – A video-like player for Claude Code sessions 34 upvotes, 17 comments
  27. Global warming has accelerated significantly 815 upvotes, 798 comments
  28. We might all be AI engineers now 128 upvotes, 179 comments
  29. Launch HN: Palus Finance (YC W26): Better yields on idle cash for startups, SMBs 21 upvotes, 31 comments
  30. Multifactor (YC F25) Is Hiring an Engineering Lead 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 5:00am, 7 March 2026. Source here.
